reasoning: CD3D and CD3E are canonical T cell receptor complex genes confirming T cell identity. LTB and LDHB are well-established markers of naïve T cells in PBMC datasets. The abundance of ribosomal proteins (RPS/RPL) and absence of cytotoxic markers (no GZMA, GZMB, PRF1) or CD8A strongly suggest naïve CD4+ T cells rather than effector subsets.
database_support: 4/10 markers (CD3D, CD3E, LTB, LDHB) were confirmed in PanglaoDB and/or CellMarker CD4+ T cell lists. The remaining markers are ribosomal housekeeping genes not expected in curated marker databases. Both databases list CD3D, CD3E, and LTB as CD4+ T cell markers, supporting this annotation.

markers_used: LYZ, S100A9, S100A8, FCN1, TYROBP, CST3
reasoning: LYZ, S100A9, and S100A8 are textbook CD14+ monocyte markers in PBMC. FCN1 is a classical monocyte-specific gene confirmed by gene search to map to monocytes in both PanglaoDB and CellMarker. TYROBP encodes DAP12, a myeloid signaling adaptor. CST3 (cystatin C) is a known myeloid marker. This is the classic CD14+ classical monocyte signature.
database_support: Direct cell-type search confirmed LYZ and S100A9 (2/10). Gene-based searches for FCN1, S100A8, and TYROBP all returned Monocytes/Classical monocytes as top hits in both PanglaoDB and CellMarker, bringing effective confirmation to 5/10 markers. Databases strongly agree this is a monocyte cluster.

markers_used: CD79A, CD79B, MS4A1, CD74, HLA-DRA, HLA-DQA1
reasoning: CD79A and CD79B are components of the B cell receptor complex, and MS4A1 (CD20) is the canonical B cell surface marker. These three genes are textbook-perfect B cell identifiers. The high expression of MHC class II genes (HLA-DRA, HLA-DQA1, HLA-DPB1, etc.) and CD74 further support antigen-presenting B cell identity in PBMC.
database_support: 10/10 markers were confirmed in PanglaoDB and/or CellMarker B cell lists. CD79A, CD79B, MS4A1, CD74, HLA-DRA, HLA-DQA1, HLA-DPB1, HLA-DQB1, HLA-DRB1, and HLA-DPA1 are all present. This is a textbook-perfect match.

markers_used: CD8A, CD3D, CCL5, NKG7, GZMA, GZMK
reasoning: CD8A combined with CD3D unambiguously identifies CD8+ T cells. CCL5 and NKG7 are hallmarks of cytotoxic lymphocytes. GZMA and GZMK (granzymes A and K) indicate effector/memory CD8+ T cell functionality. The presence of GZMK specifically (rather than GZMB) suggests these are primarily memory/effector-memory CD8+ T cells rather than terminally differentiated cytotoxic cells.
database_support: 9/10 markers (CCL5, NKG7, CST7, GZMA, GZMK, CTSW, IL32, CD8A, CD3D) were confirmed in PanglaoDB and/or CellMarker CD8+ T cell lists. Only LYAR was absent. Both databases strongly support CD8+ T cell identity.

markers_used: NKG7, GNLY, PRF1, GZMB, FGFBP2, SPON2
reasoning: NKG7, GNLY (granulysin), PRF1 (perforin), and GZMB (granzyme B) define a highly cytotoxic lymphocyte profile. FGFBP2 and SPON2 are specifically associated with NK cells rather than CD8+ T cells in PanglaoDB. The absence of CD3D/CD3E as top markers and the presence of CD247 (CD3 zeta chain, shared by NK and T cells) together with FGFBP2/SPON2 distinguishes this cluster as NK cells.
database_support: 9/10 markers (NKG7, GZMB, GNLY, PRF1, FGFBP2, CTSW, CST7, GZMA, SPON2) were confirmed in PanglaoDB NK cell marker lists. Only CD247 was not in the NK-specific list (though it is a known NK/T cell gene). Databases strongly support NK cell identity.

markers_used: FCGR3A, LST1, AIF1, FCER1G, IFITM2, IFITM3
reasoning: FCGR3A (CD16) is the defining marker of non-classical/CD16+ monocytes. LST1 and AIF1 are well-known markers of this monocyte subset, confirmed by gene searches to associate with monocytes/dendritic cells. FCER1G is a myeloid signaling adaptor. IFITM2 and IFITM3 (interferon-induced genes) are characteristically elevated in CD16+ monocytes. The absence of S100A8/A9 and presence of FCGR3A clearly distinguishes this from the CD14+ monocyte cluster.
database_support: Direct cell-type search for CD16+ monocytes confirmed FCGR3A (1/10). Gene searches for LST1, AIF1, and FCER1G all returned monocytes as a top associated cell type, bringing total support to 4/10. The limited database entries for CD16+ monocytes are a known gap; the marker profile is highly consistent with published scRNA-seq annotations of FCGR3A+ monocytes.

markers_used: HLA-DRA, HLA-DPB1, HLA-DPA1, CST3, CD74, HLA-DMA
reasoning: This cluster shows extremely high MHC class II expression (HLA-DRA, HLA-DPB1, HLA-DPA1, HLA-DRB1, HLA-DQA1) combined with CST3 and CD74, which are hallmarks of professional antigen-presenting dendritic cells. HLA-DMA, involved in antigen loading, further supports dendritic cell identity. The absence of B cell markers (CD79A, MS4A1) and monocyte markers (LYZ, S100A8) distinguishes this from B cells and monocytes.
database_support: 9/10 markers (HLA-DPB1, HLA-DPA1, HLA-DRA, CST3, CD74, HLA-DRB1, HLA-DQA1, HLA-DMA, HLA-DRB5) were confirmed in PanglaoDB and/or CellMarker dendritic cell lists. Only LSP1 was absent. Databases strongly support dendritic cell identity.

markers_used: PPBP, PF4, GP9, GNG11, SPARC, NRGN
reasoning: PPBP (pro-platelet basic protein/CXCL7) and PF4 (platelet factor 4) are the most canonical platelet markers. GP9 is a platelet glycoprotein essential for platelet adhesion. GNG11, SPARC, NRGN, CD9, and GPX1 are all well-established platelet/megakaryocyte markers. This is a textbook-perfect platelet signature commonly seen as a small cluster in PBMC datasets.
database_support: 8/10 markers (PPBP, PF4, GPX1, GNG11, SPARC, CD9, NRGN, GP9) were confirmed in PanglaoDB and/or CellMarker platelet lists. SDPR (CAVIN2) is also listed in PanglaoDB under its newer symbol. Only CALM3 was absent. Databases strongly confirm platelet identity.
